# WARNING: This file was generated by Claude Code

# Findings: why moving bubbles + two-way coupling differ across MPI rank counts

Campaign run 2026-07-05 on wingtip-gpu3 (nvfortran 25.11, CPU+MPI and OpenACC
builds), 50³ cells, 64 Keller-Miksis bubbles, tri-periodic, 10× central
pressure sphere. Raw logs: `phase{0,1,2,3}.log`; outputs: `out_*/`.

## Verdict

**There is no decomposition bug in the moving-bubble/two-way-coupling path.**
The rank-count discrepancy is a one-ulp (2.2e-16) difference in the two-way
smearing deposition wherever a bubble's kernel straddles a subdomain boundary
— a floating-point summation-order effect in the halo-accumulate reduce —
which the strongly chaotic coupled-bubble dynamics then amplify to O(1) over
a few hundred steps.

## Evidence

| test | result |
|---|---|
| P0: same config run twice (CPU 1r, CPU 8r, GPU 1r, GPU 4r) | all bitwise identical — comparisons well-posed, no GPU-atomics noise (deposition is a cell-centric gather) |
| P1 M0: no bubbles, 1v2 & 1v8 ranks, 20 steps | **bitwise identical** — Eulerian solver decomposition-exact |
| P1 M2: moving + one-way | **bitwise identical**, fields and particle trajectories |
| P1 M1: static + two-way | 16–65 cells differ by 1 ulp at step 1; ~4e-14 by step 20 |
| P1 M3: moving + two-way | same seed as M1 (16/65 cells, 1 ulp); ~4e-13 by step 20 (coupling feeds back) |
| P3 interior: single bubble, kernel away from boundary, two-way, 100 steps | **bitwise identical** — deposition itself is exact |
| P3 straddle: single bubble on the 2-rank boundary | 1-ulp seed the moment the kernel touches the boundary; ~1e-13 after 100 steps |
| P3 migrate: single bubble crossing ranks mid-run | first diff (1e-16) when kernel reaches boundary, **no jump at the crossing**; ~1.4e-14 after crossing — handoff exact to round-off |
| P2 chaos baseline: 8r vs 8r with 1e-14 IC perturbation, 500 steps | smooth exponential growth, e-folding time ≈ 0.38, punctuated burst once amplitude reaches ~1e-8 |
| P2 rank divergence: 8r vs 1r identical ICs | starts at 1 ulp, same growth character, bursts at the same ~1e-7 amplitude (reached earlier), saturates at O(1) positions by t≈7 |
| Kahan probe: M1 with `lag_params%kahan_summation=T` | identical 1-ulp seed — Kahan cannot equalize different summation orders |

The Phase 2 "bursts" occur at a divergence *amplitude* threshold (~1e-8–1e-7),
not at a fixed time: collapse/rebound timing shifts by a step once the
perturbation is large enough. The single-bubble migrate test rules out a
migration kick as the cause.

## Practical implications

- Cross-rank-count validation of two-way EL cases must be statistical
  (integrals, spectra, ensemble envelopes), not trajectory-wise: trajectories
  decorrelate at chaos rate (e-folding ~0.4 nondim time here) from an
  irreducible 1-ulp seed.
- Bitwise decomposition invariance would require a canonical-order reduction
  in `s_mpi_reduce_beta_variables_buffers` (accumulate straddling-kernel
  contributions in a fixed global order) — a performance/complexity trade,
  not a correctness fix.
- One-way coupling (`solver_approach=1`) is bitwise decomposition-invariant
  and remains a good regression anchor.
